verses.text: The twelve sons of Israel are named — the whole covenant family. After the sweep through the nations, the genealogy arrives at its true subject. From all mankind, God chose this one family to carry His promise.

The twelve sons of Israel are named — the whole covenant family. After the sweep through the nations, the genealogy arrives at its true subject. From all mankind, God chose this one family to carry His promise.

Judah's first sons are named, including Er whom the LORD slew for wickedness, and Pharez born of Tamar. The chosen tribe's history begins in failure and scandal. Yet through this flawed line God will bring David and the Messiah.

Judah's first sons are named, including Er whom the LORD slew for wickedness, and Pharez born of Tamar. The chosen tribe's history begins in failure and scandal. Yet through this flawed line God will bring David and the Messiah.

Judah's first sons are named, including Er whom the LORD slew for wickedness, and Pharez born of Tamar. The chosen tribe's history begins in failure and scandal. Yet through this flawed line God will bring David and the Messiah.

The sons of Zerah and the line of Achar, 'the troubler of Israel,' are recorded. Even a notorious sinner is named in the sacred roll. The Chronicler hides nothing; grace, not a spotless record, carries the chosen tribe.

The sons of Zerah and the line of Achar, 'the troubler of Israel,' are recorded. Even a notorious sinner is named in the sacred roll. The Chronicler hides nothing; grace, not a spotless record, carries the chosen tribe.

The sons of Zerah and the line of Achar, 'the troubler of Israel,' are recorded. Even a notorious sinner is named in the sacred roll. The Chronicler hides nothing; grace, not a spotless record, carries the chosen tribe.

Hezron's line runs down through Ram, Amminadab, and Nahshon to Boaz and Obed. The thread moves steadily toward David. Names from the book of Ruth appear, showing how God wove even a Moabite's faith into the royal line.

Jesse's sons are named, and David appears as the seventh — the goal toward which the whole genealogy has been pressing. All the long lists converge here. From Adam through Judah, God was tracing the line to the man after His own heart.

Caleb son of Hezron's children are named, down through Hur. The wider Judahite clans branch out beyond the royal line. God's record reaches past the kings to the many ordinary families that made up the tribe.

Caleb son of Hezron's children are named, down through Hur. The wider Judahite clans branch out beyond the royal line. God's record reaches past the kings to the many ordinary families that made up the tribe.

Caleb son of Hezron's children are named, down through Hur. The wider Judahite clans branch out beyond the royal line. God's record reaches past the kings to the many ordinary families that made up the tribe.

Hezron's later descendants — Segub, Jair with his towns in Gilead, and Ashur father of Tekoa — are listed. Even sons born in a patriarch's old age are recorded. God misses no branch of His people's tree.

The line of Jerahmeel, Hezron's firstborn, begins to be traced. The eldest branch is given its due. God honours the order of families even as His promise runs on through a younger line.
The line of Jerahmeel, Hezron's firstborn, begins to be traced. The eldest branch is given its due. God honours the order of families even as His promise runs on through a younger line.

Jerahmeel's line continues, noting men like Seled who 'died without children.' The branches that bore no fruit are still named. God keeps account even of the lines that ended, for none of His people is forgotten.

Sheshan, having only daughters, gives one to his Egyptian servant Jarha, and the line continues through them. A foreigner is grafted into Judah. Faith and covenant, not bloodline alone, marked the people of God.

Sheshan, having only daughters, gives one to his Egyptian servant Jarha, and the line continues through them. A foreigner is grafted into Judah. Faith and covenant, not bloodline alone, marked the people of God.

The descendants of Jarha and Sheshan's daughter are traced for many generations down to Elishama. The household of a foreign servant is honoured in the sacred roll. God's grace makes the outsider an heir among His people.

Caleb's descendants are listed, many of them place-names — Ziph, Hebron, Maon. The genealogy doubles as a map of Judah's towns. God tied His people to a real inheritance in the land He gave them.

Caleb's sons by his concubines are named, and his daughter Achsah is remembered. Even children of secondary standing, and a daughter, have their place. God's record is wider and more tender than human reckoning.

The sons of Caleb son of Hur include the founders of Bethlehem and Kirjath-jearim. The towns of future glory are quietly established. God plants in a plain genealogy the seedbeds of His later works.
The sons of Caleb son of Hur include the founders of Bethlehem and Kirjath-jearim. The towns of future glory are quietly established. God plants in a plain genealogy the seedbeds of His later works.
The sons of Caleb son of Hur include the founders of Bethlehem and Kirjath-jearim. The towns of future glory are quietly established. God plants in a plain genealogy the seedbeds of His later works.

The clans of Kirjath-jearim, the families of scribes, and the Kenites who came of Rechab close the chapter. Even scribes and the faithful Rechabites are noted. God honours those who preserve His word and keep their vows.
